workLog.detail: Maintainer accepted ADR-063 (memory-skill-decomposition) on 2026-06-17. This maintainer authorization satisfies the ADR Review Protocol gate for the status flip from proposed to accepted; the architect agent debate-round artifacts are superseded by the explicit human decision per AGENTS.md Ask-First authority. No fabricated debate log is appended. Recorded as architect-review evidence so the routing gate reflects the real authorization, not a manufactured one.

workLog.action: Phase 1 scope decision per ADR-063 Decision
workLog.detail: ADR-063 Decision point 1 binds the split axis to operation, not tier (overriding issue #1948 body probable-shape tier guess). Smallest reversible slice the ADR explicitly commits to: extract the Tier 1 search operation into a memory-search sub-skill while memory survives as the thin router (Decision points 1, 2, 5). The router-vs-dedicated-sibling location of the Memory-First Gate (Decision point 3) is left open by the ADR for later debate, so Phase 1 does not touch it. Remainder (episode/causal/maintenance sub-skills, gate location, eval/mirror regen, AC4-AC10) flagged in PR body. Refs #1948, not Fixes.

nextSteps: Phase 2 of #1948: decide Memory-First Gate location (ADR-063 Decision point 3, router vs memory-gate sibling).
Regenerate tests/evals/skills/triage-prompts.json (AC5) and rerun eval-knowledge-integration kill gate (AC6).
Regenerate src/copilot-cli/skills mirror (AC7); full memory*/ vendor-ref sweep (AC8).
